explicit_text: I grew up in Modesto, California, in a financially poor family. It was my hometown and the place I returned to after starting at Berkeley during the Great Depression. I also attended Modesto Junior College there when I had to pause my studies due to financial difficulties.

explicit_text: My family originally came from the remote western mountains of Colorado. Although I grew up in Modesto, California, my roots trace back to that rugged region. It was part of my family background before we settled in California.

explicit_text: I began my studies at the University of California, Berkeley, during the early days of the Great Depression. After a financial setback, I returned there thanks to a scholarship from my teachers. I graduated in 1937 with the University Medal, the highest student honor, and later joined the Berkeley faculty in electrical engineering in 1946, eventually becoming University Professor Emeritus.

explicit_text: My first professional job was at General Electric, where I worked on microwave components, electron tubes, and quantum electron devices. I developed innovative solutions and contributed to advances in microwave circuits and traveling-wave tubes used in communication satellites. It was there I collaborated with Simon Ramo and helped pioneer important research in electronics.

explicit_text: While at General Electric, I collaborated with Simon Ramo on developing a pioneering textbook on microwaves. entity: Fields and Waves in Communication Electronics
explicit_text: I co-developed Fields and Waves in Communication Electronics with Simon Ramo while working at General Electric. It reflects the foundational work we did on microwave circuits and devices.
